# Schema reference — every kind's `spec`, generated

<!-- GENERATED by tools/gen_schema_ref.py from src/sre_kb/schemas/ — do not edit by hand.
     Regenerate with `make schema-ref`; CI fails if this file is stale. -->

One row per field of each registered kind's `spec`, rendered from the same JSON Schemas the
validator enforces (`additionalProperties: false` — fields not listed here are rejected).
A nested field marked required is required *within its parent object* when that parent is
present. Skills and reviews should link here rather than restating shapes in prose.

## Flow

A user/business-visible request flow: trigger, the ordered steps through code and dependencies (each with its failure modes), terminal sinks, and the SLO that governs it. The unit blast radius, alerts, and runbooks key on.

| field | type | required | notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `trigger` | object | yes |  |
| `trigger.type` | string | yes |  |
| `trigger.method` | string or null |  |  |
| `trigger.path` | string or null |  |  |
| `trigger.entrypoint` | string or null |  |  |
| `steps` | array | yes |  |
| `steps[].id` | string | yes |  |
| `steps[].name` | string | yes |  |
| `steps[].kind` | string | yes |  |
| `steps[].failureModes` | array |  |  |
| `sinks` | array |  |  |
| `sloRef` | string |  |  |

## ResiliencyPattern

One resilience mechanism present in code (breaker/retry/timeout/bulkhead/rate-limiter...): the library, the symbol it guards, and its fallback where declared. The presence half the gap-finder's absence claims are checked against.

| field | type | required | notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `type` | string | yes | one of: `circuit-breaker`, `retry`, `bulkhead`, `timeout`, `rate-limit` |
| `library` | string | yes |  |
| `targetSymbol` | string or null |  |  |
| `fallbackMethod` | string or null |  |  |

## Observability

What the service emits and exposes: logging (framework, format, statement quality, correlation context), metrics, tracing, health indicators, and actuator exposure.

| field | type | required | notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `logging` | object | yes |  |
| `logging.framework` | string | yes |  |
| `logging.format` | string | yes |  |
| `logging.pattern` | string or null |  |  |
| `logging.correlationFields` | array of string |  |  |
| `logging.statements` | object |  | Roll-up of the log statements parsed from code (S2 assess-logging). |
| `logging.statements.total` | integer |  |  |
| `logging.statements.byLevel` | object |  |  |
| `logging.statements.loggingApis` | array of string |  |  |
| `logging.statements.parameterized` | integer |  |  |
| `logging.quality` | object |  | Deterministic logging-quality assessment (S2 assess-logging): request/trace-ID context and alert-fatigue signals. |
| `logging.quality.correlationContext` | boolean |  |  |
| `logging.quality.correlationFields` | array of string |  |  |
| `logging.quality.placeholderHygiene` | number or null |  |  |
| `logging.quality.alertFatigueSignals` | array of string |  |  |
| `metrics` | array |  |  |
| `tracing` | object or string or null |  |  |
| `healthIndicators` | array |  |  |
| `actuatorEndpoints` | array of string |  |  |

## Fallback

A built-in degradation path: what triggers it, the symbol that implements it, and the behavior the caller gets instead of the failure (e.g. a breaker's fallbackMethod).

| field | type | required | notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `trigger` | string |  |  |
| `fallbackSymbol` | string or null |  |  |
| `behavior` | string | yes |  |
| `forTarget` | string or null |  |  |

## Alert

An alerting rule grounded in the scan: SLO burn-rate (multi-window/multi-burn-rate on an ingested objective) or a log-pattern alert keyed on a parsed log statement. Rendered per backend by the alert adapters; `expr` carries the tool-neutral intent, never a hand-written query.

| field | type | required | notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `alertType` | string | yes | one of: `burn-rate`, `threshold` |
| `sloRef` | string or null |  |  |
| `signalSource` | string | yes | one of: `log-pattern`, `metric` |
| `severity` | string |  |  |
| `forFlow` | string |  |  |
| `logFormatRef` | string or null |  |  |
| `expr` | object | yes |  |
| `rationale` | string |  |  |
| `class` | string |  | one of: `symptom`, `cause` — SRE alert class (adopted from resiliency-skills AlertIntent): symptom = user-visible SLO burn; cause = a specific failure mode (e.g. a swallowed publish). |
| `signal` | object |  | Tool-neutral description of what is observed (the intent), independent of any backend's query dialect. |
| `signal.type` | string | yes | one of: `log`, `metric`, `trace`, `synthetic` |
| `signal.metric` | string |  |  |
| `signal.route` | string or null |  |  |
| `signal.description` | string |  |  |
| `condition` | object |  | Tool-neutral threshold for a threshold-type alert. |
| `condition.comparator` | string | yes | one of: `>`, `>=`, `<`, `<=`, `==`, `!=` |
| `condition.threshold` | number or string | yes |  |
| `condition.window` | string |  |  |
| `condition.occurrences` | integer |  |  |
| `burnRate` | object |  | Structured multi-window error-budget burn-rate parameters (the intent behind the rendered `expr`). |
| `burnRate.sloRef` | string | yes |  |
| `burnRate.sli` | string |  | one of: `latency`, `availability` |
| `burnRate.shortWindow` | string | yes |  |
| `burnRate.longWindow` | string | yes |  |
| `burnRate.shortFactor` | number |  |  |
| `burnRate.longFactor` | number |  |  |
| `burnRate.budgetFraction` | number |  |  |
| `renderTargets` | array of string |  | Monitoring backends the `expr` was rendered for (adopted from AlertIntent.renderTargets). |

## Runbook

Operator guidance for one alert/failure mode: trigger, symptoms, diagnosis, remediation, and escalation. Generated content carries the verify-before-executing banner and cites the scanned commit.

| field | type | required | notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `banner` | string |  |  |
| `trigger` | object | yes |  |
| `trigger.alertRef` | string | yes |  |
| `symptoms` | array of string |  |  |
| `diagnosis` | array |  |  |
| `remediation` | array of string | yes |  |
| `escalation` | string |  |  |
| `relatedFlow` | string |  |  |

## BlastRadius

Engine-computed failure impact for one node (dependency/broker/datastore): the flows and services degraded when it fails, containment present or absent, co-tenancy across services, and whether state makes failure lossy. Labeled a best-effort lower bound — flow gaps may hide impact.

| field | type | required | notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `node` | object | yes |  |
| `node.type` | string | yes |  |
| `node.name` | string | yes |  |
| `impactedFlows` | array of string | yes |  |
| `impactedServices` | array of string |  |  |
| `containment` | array |  |  |
| `coTenancy` | array |  |  |
| `stateful` | object |  |  |
| `stateful.dataLossRisk` | boolean |  |  |
| `dependencyCriticality` | string |  |  |
| `severityHint` | string |  | one of: `low`, `medium`, `high`, `critical` |
| `riskRationale` | string |  |  |

## SloSli

The service-level objectives for a flow: SLI, target, and window per objective, with the source recorded (an ingested sre-slo.yml catalog yields full objectives; otherwise needs-review placeholders).

| field | type | required | notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `objectives` | array | yes |  |
| `objectives[].sli` | string | yes |  |
| `objectives[].target` | string or number or null |  |  |
| `objectives[].window` | string or null |  |  |
| `source` | string | yes |  |
| `forFlow` | string |  |  |

## ReadinessScore

The production-readiness roll-up: PRR check results, the 0-100 score and grade, coverage counts, and the named gaps. Recomputed status-aware after gating, so it only credits verified coverage.

| field | type | required | notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `prrChecks` | object | yes |  |
| `score` | number | yes |  |
| `grade` | string |  |  |
| `coverage` | object |  |  |
| `gaps` | array of string |  |  |

## ServiceCatalogEntry

The Backstage-style catalog row: component type, lifecycle, owning system, APIs provided, and dependencies — the projection a catalog ingests.

| field | type | required | notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `type` | string | yes |  |
| `lifecycle` | string | yes |  |
| `system` | string |  |  |
| `providesApis` | array |  |  |
| `dependsOn` | array |  |  |

## TechStack

The detected stack: languages, frameworks, runtime, build tool, operationally notable libraries, and the PCF buildpack context.

| field | type | required | notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `languages` | array of string |  |  |
| `frameworks` | array | yes |  |
| `runtime` | string |  |  |
| `buildTool` | string |  |  |
| `notableLibraries` | array of string |  |  |
| `pcf` | object |  |  |

## Architecture

The service's architecture narrative: components, layers, and design patterns/styles in use. Largely Tier-B (LLM judgment over the scanned structure), so it lands needs-review.

| field | type | required | notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `components` | array | yes |  |
| `components[].name` | string | yes |  |
| `components[].type` | string | yes |  |
| `components[].symbol` | string |  |  |
| `layers` | array of string |  |  |
| `patterns` | array of string |  |  |
| `styleTags` | array of string |  |  |

## Deployment

The app's deployment contract with the platform (PCF manifest): instances, memory/disk, routes, bound services, stack/buildpacks, and the declared health check.

| field | type | required | notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `hosting` | string | yes | one of: `PCF`, `VM`, `cloud` |
| `instances` | integer or null |  |  |
| `memory` | string or null |  |  |
| `disk` | string or null |  |  |
| `routes` | array |  |  |
| `services` | array |  |  |
| `stack` | string or null |  |  |
| `buildpacks` | array |  |  |
| `healthCheck` | object |  |  |
| `profiles` | string or array or null |  |  |
| `processes` | array |  |  |
| `sidecars` | array |  |  |
| `environment` | string |  |  |

## Dependency

One external dependency of the service (HTTP API, datastore, broker), with where it was declared, its engine where known, and its criticality to the service's flows.

| field | type | required | notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `name` | string | yes |  |
| `type` | string | yes | one of: `datastore`, `broker`, `service-binding`, `http`, `library` |
| `source` | string |  |  |
| `baseUrl` | string or null |  |  |
| `engine` | string or null |  | Datastore/broker engine (e.g. postgres, kafka) — folded in from the former DataStore kind (S1: app binds X, not the infra). |
| `criticality` | string |  |  |

## Interface

The service's inbound surface: REST/messaging style, the concrete endpoints/channels extracted from code, and the spec contract (documented vs undocumented vs spec-only) when an OpenAPI/AsyncAPI baseline exists.

| field | type | required | notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `style` | string | yes |  |
| `endpoints` | array |  |  |
| `channels` | array |  |  |
| `contract` | object |  | Drift against an ingested OpenAPI/AsyncAPI spec (#7): undocumented = code exposes but spec omits; specOnly = spec documents but no handler serves. When a `.sre/api-baseline/` spec is committed, `baselineVersion`/`changes`/`versionPolicy` carry the deterministic breaking-change diff vs that baseline. |
| `contract.source` | string or null |  |  |
| `contract.specPath` | string or null |  |  |
| `contract.version` | string or null |  |  |
| `contract.documented` | integer |  |  |
| `contract.undocumented` | array of string |  |  |
| `contract.specOnly` | array of string |  |  |
| `contract.baselineVersion` | string or null |  |  |
| `contract.changes` | array |  | Deterministic, byte-grounded diff vs the committed baseline spec. |
| `contract.changes[].changeType` | string | yes | one of: `operation-removed`, `operation-added`, `required-parameter-added` |
| `contract.changes[].ref` | string | yes |  |
| `contract.changes[].breaking` | boolean | yes |  |
| `contract.changes[].detail` | string or null |  |  |
| `contract.versionPolicy` | object or null |  | Semver check: a breaking change present without a major bump fails (ok=false). |
| `contract.versionPolicy.ok` | boolean |  |  |
| `contract.versionPolicy.breakingChanges` | integer |  |  |
| `contract.versionPolicy.majorBumped` | boolean |  |  |
| `contract.versionPolicy.detail` | string or null |  |  |

## Messaging

Consumer-side async-resilience posture (HYBRID-PLAN S3). The Interface kind carries the messaging *contract* (channels); this carries each consumer's *resilience* — the DLQ/retry/idempotency mechanisms detected deterministically. Ordering/partition safety, poison-pill adequacy, and saga compensation are judgment calls routed to the map-messaging skill (Tier-B), not here.

| field | type | required | notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `consumers` | array | yes |  |
| `consumers[].channel` | string | yes |  |
| `consumers[].broker` | string | yes |  |
| `consumers[].handler` | string | yes |  |
| `consumers[].resilience` | object |  |  |
| `consumers[].resilience.deadLetter` | boolean |  |  |
| `consumers[].resilience.deadLetterMechanism` | string or null |  |  |
| `consumers[].resilience.retry` | boolean |  |  |
| `consumers[].resilience.idempotentConsumer` | boolean |  |  |

## ScheduledJob

A recurring/background job (adopted from resiliency-skills' jobs schema, on our byte-grounded envelope). One artifact per detected job, cited to its scheduling site.

| field | type | required | notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `name` | string | yes |  |
| `jobType` | string | yes | one of: `cron`, `batch`, `worker`, `scheduled` |
| `schedule` | string |  |  |
| `trigger` | string |  |  |
| `timeoutSeconds` | integer |  |  |
| `concurrencyPolicy` | string |  | one of: `allow`, `forbid`, `replace` |
| `expectedDuration` | string |  |  |

A delivery or deployment pipeline relevant to service readiness.

| field | type | required | notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `name` | string | yes |  |
| `system` | string |  |  |
| `repository` | string |  |  |
| `branch` | string |  |  |
| `stages` | array of string |  |  |
| `promotion` | string |  |  |
| `rollback` | string |  |  |

## Topology

The app-centric dependency graph: services and the datastores/brokers/externals they bind or call, as nodes and typed edges. Estate runs merge several services into one shared topology.

| field | type | required | notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `nodes` | array | yes |  |
| `nodes[].type` | string | yes |  |
| `nodes[].name` | string | yes |  |
| `edges` | array | yes |  |
| `edges[].from` | string | yes |  |
| `edges[].to` | string | yes |  |
| `edges[].relation` | string |  |  |
| `pcfSpaces` | array |  |  |

## FeatureFlag

A feature flag or runtime toggle that affects operational behavior.

| field | type | required | notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `name` | string | yes |  |
| `provider` | string |  |  |
| `defaultState` | string |  | one of: `on`, `off`, `unknown` |
| `owner` | string |  |  |
| `blastRadius` | string |  |  |
| `killSwitch` | boolean |  |  |

## ConfigManagement

How the service is configured: config sources (files/env/config-server), active profiles, refresh scope, and the operationally notable properties extracted from them.

| field | type | required | notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `sources` | array of string | yes |  |
| `profiles` | array |  |  |
| `refreshScope` | boolean |  |  |
| `properties` | array |  |  |

## Dashboard

A monitoring dashboard (adopted from resiliency-skills' dashboard schema, on our byte-grounded envelope). Panels carry a tool-neutral signal with a generated per-source query.

| field | type | required | notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `title` | string | yes |  |
| `renderTarget` | string |  | one of: `prometheus`, `splunk`, `wavefront`, `appdynamics`, `grafana` |
| `panels` | array | yes |  |
| `panels[].title` | string | yes |  |
| `panels[].type` | string | yes | one of: `timeseries`, `stat`, `gauge`, `table`, `heatmap` |
| `panels[].unit` | string |  |  |
| `panels[].signal` | object | yes |  |
| `panels[].signal.source` | string | yes | one of: `prometheus`, `splunk`, `wavefront`, `appdynamics`, `grafana` |
| `panels[].signal.metric` | string |  |  |
| `panels[].signal.query` | string |  |  |
| `panels[].signal.description` | string |  |  |

## Criticality

Service criticality + data classification (adopted from resiliency-skills' criticality schema, on our byte-grounded envelope). Drives the deterministic alert severity floor and blast-radius weighting. `tier`/`businessCriticality` are declared (catalog/human) and grounded to the declaring file; `dataClassification` can be re-derived deterministically from PII/PCI signatures in code (HYBRID-PLAN Round-3 R1).

| field | type | required | notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `tier` | string | yes | one of: `tier0`, `tier1`, `tier2`, `tier3`, `unknown` |
| `businessCriticality` | string |  | one of: `critical`, `high`, `medium`, `low`, `unknown` |
| `dataClassification` | array of string |  |  |
| `source` | string | yes | one of: `catalog`, `human-input`, `inferred` |

## ResiliencyGap

A resiliency gap. Most are LLM-proposed (Tier B) then re-grounded with the shared signature library (HYBRID-PLAN §7.9): refutation-probe gaps are sourceTier=llm/needs-review; a confirmation-probe gap the engine re-derived at the pointer graduates to sourceTier=ast and may verify (§9.4). Parameter-completeness gaps (rederivation=param-completeness, Round-3 R5) are engine-only Tier-A: a resilience pattern is declared but a load-bearing param is unconfigured (e.g. a @CircuitBreaker with no failure-rate-threshold) — no LLM involved.

| field | type | required | notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `category` | string | yes | one of: `missing-timeout`, `unguarded-critical-dependency`, `swallowed-failure`, `data-loss-path`, `missing-idempotency`, `undocumented-job`, `unbounded-resource`, `circuit-breaker-without-thresholds`, `retry-without-backoff`, `missing-metrics`, `missing-tracing`, `missing-structured-logging`, `missing-synthetic-monitoring`, `missing-backpressure`, `missing-load-shedding`, `missing-log-context`, `noisy-error-logging`, `consumer-without-dlq`, `non-idempotent-consumer`, `missing-poison-pill-handling`, `unordered-consumer`, `missing-saga-compensation`, `disabled-resilience`, `novel` |
| `proposedCategory` | string or null |  | For category=novel: the LLM-proposed out-of-taxonomy category name (open-discovery channel), pending graduation into the taxonomy. |
| `target` | string or null |  |  |
| `severity` | string | yes | one of: `high`, `medium`, `low` |
| `rationale` | string or null |  |  |
| `rederivation` | string | yes | one of: `confirmed`, `judgment`, `param-completeness`, `consumer-resilience`, `mutating-route`, `disabled`, `novel` |
| `sourceTier` | string | yes | one of: `llm`, `ast` |
| `checked` | array of string |  | Paths the engine searched for a refuting signature before asserting the absence (honest negative). |

## SecurityPosture

Security controls and review notes that affect operational readiness.

| field | type | required | notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `controls` | array of string | yes |  |
| `secrets` | string |  |  |
| `authn` | string |  |  |
| `authz` | string |  |  |
| `encryption` | string |  |  |
| `openRisks` | array of string |  |  |
